original_abstract: This study examines the evolving landscape of international higher education, with a specific focus on the shifting dynamics of educational policy and politics within the Romanian context. By analyzing the role of the Executive Agency for Higher Education, Research, Development and Innovation Funding (UEFISCDI), the research explores how international trends and comparative frameworks influence domestic higher education reforms. Central to this inquiry is the transition toward evidence-based policymaking and its impact on the governance of public institutions. Through a critical lens, the paper assesses how Romania navigates the pressures of the global education arena while balancing national priorities, ultimately providing insights into the complexities of modernizing higher education systems in a globalized world.
humanized_abstract: International Higher Education: Dynamics of change in policy and politics: case study: Romania The paper represents a part of a larger study aiming to map and explore the dynamics of change occurring in international higher education, specifically by examining the changes in policies and politics. It investigates how policy actors and agencies within specific countries, namely the Executive Agency for Higher Education, Research, Development and Innovation Funding (UEFISCDI), take into account the emerging trends in the field of international higher education and use the recommendations emanating from comparative studies to inform and shape national changes and developments in higher education. Using a critical perspective, this paper analyses how countries address the demands and pressures emanating from the globalisation of education, while ensuring that national goals and values are protected, and, thus, informs us on the mechanisms for modernisation of higher education systems worldwide.
